title: Data Structures & Algorithms — Java Assignment Collection
summary: A collection of data structures and algorithms assignments implemented in Java. Modules span classic data structures (union-find, linked lists, balanced trees, hash maps, deques) and their applications (percolation simulation, 2048 puzzle, Conway's Game of Life, Tetris, guitar synthesis, NGrams word-frequency analysis). Most modules are independent; four intra- and cross-module code dependencies drive the implementation order.
instructionPreview: This is a collection of assignments from an undergraduate data structures and algorithms course in Java. You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that all tests pass.
instruction:
This is a collection of assignments from an undergraduate data structures
and algorithms course in Java. You need to implement
all the modules and features listed below so that all tests pass.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.
Required JAR libraries (JUnit, Hamcrest, Google Truth, Guava) are in `/workspace/lib/`.

## Modules to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **lab03 — Adventure Game and IntList** (`lab03/src/`): A text-based adventure
  game framework with multiple stages and a linked list data structure. The
  `IntList` class provides a singly-linked list of integers with recursive
  operations. The adventure stages contain array/list utility methods that
  compute element-wise operations and similarity comparisons. (~7 files,
  ~15 functions to implement)

- **lab05 — Union-Find** (`lab05/src/UnionFind.java`): A Disjoint Set
  (Union-Find) data structure using an array-based representation. Roots
  store negative set sizes. Must support path compression and union-by-size.
  (~1 file, ~4 functions to implement)

- **lab06 — Linked-List Map** (`lab06/src/ULLMap.java`): A dictionary
  implemented as an unordered linked list of key-value pairs. Supports
  get, put, containsKey, size, clear, and iteration. (~1 file, ~6 functions
  to implement)

- **lab07 — Red-Black Tree** (`lab07/src/RedBlackTree.java`): A left-leaning
  Red-Black BST supporting insert with color flips and rotations. (~1 file,
  ~4 functions to implement)

- **lab08 — Hash Map** (`lab08/src/hashmap/MyHashMap.java`): A hash table
  using separate chaining with dynamic resizing based on a load factor.
  Implements the Map61B interface. (~1 file, ~8 functions to implement)

- **lab09 — Game of Life** (`lab09/src/gameoflife/GameOfLife.java`): An
  implementation of Conway's Game of Life on a tile grid. Includes board
  state evolution, matrix transformations (flip, transpose), random tile
  generation, and file I/O for saving/loading board state. (~1 file,
  ~8 functions to implement)

- **lab10 — Tetris** (`lab10/src/tetris/`): A Tetris game engine with piece
  movement, collision detection, rotation, line clearing, scoring, and a
  game loop. The `Movement` class handles piece movement and rotation
  validation. The `Tetris` class handles game state and rendering.
  (~2 files, ~10 functions to implement)

- **hw2 — Percolation** (`hw2/src/Percolation.java`): A percolation system
  using weighted quick-union-find. Supports opening sites, checking if sites
  are open/full, counting open sites, and determining if the system percolates.
  (~1 file, ~6 functions to implement)

- **proj0 — 2048 Game** (`proj0/src/game2048logic/Model.java`): The core
  game logic for 2048 including tile movement, merging, game-over detection,
  and board state queries. (~1 file, ~6 functions to implement)

- **proj1c — Deque and Guitar** (`proj1c/src/`): Two deque implementations
  (linked-list and array-based) implementing the Deque61B interface, plus a
  guitar string simulator using the Karplus-Strong algorithm. (~3 files,
  ~15 functions to implement)

- **proj2a — NGrams** (`proj2a/src/ngrams/`): A time-series data structure
  and an NGram map for analyzing word frequency data from Google NGrams.
  Supports count histories, weight histories, and summed weight histories
  over year ranges. (~2 files, ~12 functions to implement)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must
implement them with these exact signatures:

- `UnionFind.sizeOf(int v)` — returns the size of the set containing v
- `UnionFind.connected(int v1, int v2)` — returns true if v1 and v2 are in the same set
- `UnionFind.find(int v)` — returns the root of the set containing v
- `UnionFind.union(int v1, int v2)` — merges the sets of v1 and v2

moduleDag.project: Data Structures & Algorithms — Java Assignment Collection
moduleDag.description: A collection of data structures and algorithms assignments implemented in Java. Modules span classic data structures (union-find, linked lists, balanced trees, hash maps, deques) and their applications (percolation simulation, 2048 puzzle, Conway's Game of Life, Tetris, guitar synthesis, NGrams word-frequency analysis). Most modules are independent; four intra- and cross-module code dependencies drive the implementation order.

nodes.label: lab03 — IntList
nodes.description: A singly-linked list of integers with recursive operations. Provides get(i), of(int...), equals(), and print() as foundational primitives used by PalindromeStage in the same lab.

nodes.label: lab03 — Adventure Game Stages
nodes.description: A text-based adventure game framework with five playable stages: BeeCountingStage (array summing), MachineStage (elementwise max/sum), PalindromeStage (IntList reversal and digit parsing), SpeciesListStage (list similarity), and AdventureGame (stage orchestration and response parsing). PalindromeStage calls IntList.of() and constructs IntList nodes.

nodes.label: lab05 — Union-Find
nodes.description: Array-based disjoint-set (union-find) with path compression and union-by-size. Roots store negative set sizes. Supports find(), union(), connected(), and sizeOf().

nodes.label: lab06 — Unordered Linked-List Map
nodes.description: A generic dictionary backed by an unordered singly-linked list of key-value Entry nodes. Implements get, put, containsKey, size, clear, and iteration by traversing the chain. No dependency on other student-implemented modules.

nodes.label: lab07 — Left-Leaning Red-Black Tree
nodes.description: A left-leaning red-black BST supporting insertion with color flips and left/right rotations to maintain balance. The insert method applies rotateLeft, rotateRight, and flipColors bottom-up after each BST insertion.

nodes.label: lab08 — Hash Map (separate chaining)
nodes.description: A hash table implementing the Map61B interface via separate chaining with LinkedList buckets. Supports dynamic resizing when the load factor exceeds the threshold. Implements put, get, containsKey, remove, size, clear, keySet, and iterator.

nodes.label: lab09 — Conway's Game of Life
nodes.description: Conway's Game of Life on a TETile grid. Implements next-generation evolution with neighbor counting, matrix flip and transpose for rotation, random board initialization, and save/load board state via file I/O.

nodes.label: lab10 — Tetris Movement
nodes.description: Low-level Tetris piece movement and rotation logic. Handles collision detection (canMove, canRotate), lateral and downward movement (tryMove, dropDown), and piece rotation (left/right) using shape matrix transposition. Tetris.java calls these methods in its game loop.

nodes.label: lab10 — Tetris Game Engine
nodes.description: Full Tetris game loop: keyboard input handling, line-clearing with downward row shifting, score accumulation, game-over detection, piece spawning, board rendering, and score display. Delegates all movement validation and execution to the Movement class.

nodes.label: hw2 — Percolation
nodes.description: An N×N percolation model using two WeightedQuickUnionUF instances (one with a virtual bottom node to prevent backwash). Supports open(), isOpen(), isFull(), numberOfOpenSites(), and percolates(). The WeightedQuickUnionUF library is pre-existing; no student-implemented modules are required.

nodes.label: proj0 — 2048 Game Logic
nodes.description: Core logic for the 2048 puzzle: emptySpaceExists, maxTileExists, and atLeastOneMoveExists for game-state queries; moveTileUpAsFarAsPossible, tiltColumn, and tilt for tile movement and merging in any of the four cardinal directions.

nodes.label: proj1c — Deque Implementations
nodes.description: Two complete implementations of the Deque61B double-ended queue interface: LinkedListDeque61B (doubly-linked list with sentinel node) and ArrayDeque61B (circular array with automatic resizing). Both support addFirst/addLast, removeFirst/removeLast, get, iterator, equals, and toString.

nodes.label: proj1c — GuitarString (Karplus-Strong)
nodes.description: Guitar string simulation using the Karplus-Strong algorithm. Declares its ring buffer as Deque61B<Double> and instantiates ArrayDeque61B directly. pluck() fills the buffer with white noise, tic() advances the simulation with decay, and sample() reads the current output value.

nodes.label: proj2a — TimeSeries
nodes.description: A sorted map from year (Integer) to count (Double) extending TreeMap. Provides a range-copy constructor, years(), data(), plus() (year-union addition), and dividedBy() (elementwise division) for time-series arithmetic used by NGramMap.

nodes.label: proj2a — NGramMap
nodes.description: Parses Google NGrams word-frequency and total-counts files into a Map<String, TimeSeries> and a total-counts TimeSeries. Provides countHistory, weightHistory (counts divided by total via TimeSeries.dividedBy), and summedWeightHistory (via TimeSeries.plus), all with optional year-range filtering.

edges.label: PalindromeStage.reverseList() constructs IntList nodes and digitsToIntList() calls IntList.of(); these methods cannot compile without IntList implemented
Tetris.updateBoard() calls movement.tryMove() and movement.rotateRight(); the interactive game loop cannot function until Movement is implemented
GuitarString imports deque.ArrayDeque61B and declares its buffer as Deque61B<Double>; the file will not compile until the deque implementations exist
NGramMap stores Map<String, TimeSeries> and calls TimeSeries.plus() and dividedBy() in weightHistory and summedWeightHistory; correct results require TimeSeries implemented first
